business_justification: ESXi cluster node hosts 18 production VMs including payment processing and customer database. Patch requires cluster rolling upgrade with VM live migration. vMotion bandwidth insufficient for simultaneous migration of all workloads. Scheduled for January 11, 2026 maintenance window.
compensating_controls: 1. ESXi management interface restricted to dedicated management VLAN (10.50.100.0/24) accessible only via MFA-protected jump box. 2. CrowdStrike Falcon sensors on all 18 guest VMs with behavioral detection and network containment enabled. 3. Palo Alto IDS/IPS signatures deployed for CVE-2025-22224 exploit patterns (Threat ID 95823). 4. VMware Tools guest isolation and VBS-based security features enabled. 5. Daily automated VM snapshots for rapid recovery.
review_notes: Q4 revalidation completed December 28 by Security Engineering. All 5 compensating controls verified: management VLAN ACLs confirmed (fw audit Dec 27), CrowdStrike sensors active on all 18 VMs (dashboard screenshot retained), IDS signatures active (0 hits — no exploitation detected), guest isolation settings verified in vCenter, snapshot job running successfully. Remediation on track for January 11.

business_justification: Internal firewall in HA active-passive cluster. Patch requires HA failover which disrupts stateful inspection for 30-60 seconds. Production traffic traverses this firewall. Maintenance window requires business stakeholder approval for brief outage. Scheduled for January 11, 2026 maintenance window.
compensating_controls: 1. FortiGate management interface access restricted to management VLAN — not reachable from general production or corporate networks. 2. Admin access requires MFA via FortiToken. 3. Local administrator accounts disabled — all access via RADIUS to Okta with MFA. 4. Network IDS monitoring management VLAN traffic for authentication bypass indicators. 5. FortiManager configuration audit logging enabled with Splunk forwarding.
review_notes: Q4 revalidation completed December 28 by Network Engineering. All controls verified: management interface ACLs confirmed, FortiToken MFA operational, local admin accounts disabled (config audit attached), IDS signatures active, FortiManager logs flowing to Splunk. NOTE: Compensating control validation was performed by the same team that owns the asset (Network Engineering) rather than by an independent team. Previous revalidation was also performed by Network Engineering on initial approval.

business_justification: Legacy customer portal built on Struts 2.x. Framework-level vulnerability requires application rewrite. Portal retirement planned for Q2 2026 as part of migration to React-based frontend.
compensating_controls: 1. Application not internet-facing — internal access via VPN only. 2. WAF rules (Imperva) with Struts exploitation signatures. 3. Application runs with minimal database privileges. 4. Input validation middleware deployed at reverse proxy.
review_notes: Q3 revalidation November 18. All controls operational. Migration project in requirements phase.

business_justification: D-Link NAS for non-critical backup staging. End-of-life — no vendor patch available. Hardware replacement approved for Q1 2026.
compensating_controls: 1. Isolated VLAN — no internet access. 2. ACL restricts access to 4 named users. 3. No sensitive or regulated data. 4. Daily replication to S3.
review_notes: Q4 revalidation December 5. Device isolated. Replacement ordered.
